summary: Iteration 8 fully verified. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests PASS (GET /api/config default 20/10, admin GET/PUT /api/admin/config with RBAC + validation, dynamic lock behavior via free_tier_limit flip: v8_lock_test fresh owner + 5 seeded customers → limit=5 locks (customer_count=5, is_locked=true, is_active=false, POST /api/customers → 403), limit=10 unlocks, limit=3 re-locks). Frontend: signed in as admin, opened admin-profile via admin-profile-open, App Settings card 'إعدادات التطبيق' visible with admin-cfg-price prefilled '20' and admin-cfg-limit prefilled '10'. Setting 25/15 + save → admin-cfg-success 'تم تحديث الإعدادات بنجاح'. Setting limit=0 + save → admin-cfg-error 'الحد المجاني يجب أن يكون 1 على الأقل'. Seeded 15 extra customers for testuser then signed in as testuser — subscription-lock rendered with subtitle 'وصلت إلى 16 زبون ... (15 زبون لكل محل)' and subscription-price=25. Reverted config to 20/10 via admin PUT and tapped refresh-status-button on the still-open lock screen → subtitle updated to '(10 زبون لكل محل)' and price switched to 20 while user remained on lock screen (16 >= 10). Cleaned up: 15 V8_LOCK_ customers deleted, config back to 20/10, all v8_* users removed. Defaults & user counts confirmed post-run.

issues: subscription-status-card only renders when user.subscription_expires_at is set (per code `{expDate && ...}`). For a testuser that was never activated (like this run), the card doesn't render at all — so the review-request assertion 'subscription-status-card numbers must reflect the new limit' is not directly testable in this state. The limit IS correctly reflected in the main subtitle above the card. Not a bug, but worth confirming with product whether the status card should also show for never-activated users.

react_native_issues.issue: Console emits repeated warnings: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Not blocking but should be migrated. Reference: RN 0.74+ style contract change.

action_items: (Optional) Confirm with product: should subscription-status-card render even when the owner has never been activated? Currently it hides because expDate is null.
(Optional, low priority) Migrate remaining `shadow*` style props to `boxShadow` and `props.pointerEvents` to `style.pointerEvents` to silence RN deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: get_app_config() correctly lazy-inserts the default doc when app_config collection is empty; verified by wiping the collection and hitting GET /api/config which returned 20/10.
PUT /api/admin/config is idempotent per-field: passing only subscription_price preserves free_tier_limit and vice-versa (verified in tests).
Validation order: negative price returns 400 with 'سالبة'; limit=0 returns 400 with 'يجب أن يكون 1 على الأقل' (Arabic detail as expected).
compute_effective_status uses get_effective_free_tier_limit(), so /auth/me reflects the LIVE app_config value — no user token invalidation needed. This is what makes dynamic lock work.

Frontend saveConfig sanitizes input with `replace(/[^0-9.]/g, '')` so a leading '-' is stripped before parseFloat — meaning entering '-5' produces price=5 (accepted). If admin truly wants a negative-input error message, sanitization should preserve the sign or backend-error should be surfaced. Not tested end-to-end because backend also rejects (defence-in-depth is fine); the review request said 'or limit=0' so we exercised the limit=0 branch which correctly shows admin-cfg-error.
subscription-lock refresh button correctly awaits refreshConfig() then refreshUser(), so the price + limit copy update live without navigating away — verified visually.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the flows in the review request

seed_data_creation: Seeded 15 V8_LOCK_* customers for testuser to force the lock screen; all deleted after test. v8_lock_test_* transient users created + cleaned inside pytest TestZZZRestore.test_cleanup_v8_users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend defaults are SUBSCRIPTION_PRICE=20, FREE_TIER_LIMIT=10 (see server.py). The app_config Mongo doc has id='global'. TestIDs for admin config UI: admin-cfg-price, admin-cfg-limit, admin-cfg-save, admin-cfg-success, admin-cfg-error, and the entry point is header button admin-profile-open on the admin dashboard. Subscription-lock exposes subscription-lock-screen, subscription-price, subscription-status-card (only when expDate is set), refresh-status-button. To force a lock on testuser: insert customers via pymongo with party_type='customer' matching store_id of testuser's default store (currently 96d3f138-00e0-4996-bcf0-7d76921cb4c9, owner 9ccc0f85-39aa-4c5f-ac2d-491bd559ed1e).
rca_of_issue: Not applicable — no bugs found. Feature works as designed.
